danger_signals.visual_form: Ride explosion/visual degradation when maintenance neglected
danger_signals.meaning: Neglected maintenance indicates system breakdown and guest danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual destruction of park assets (balloons popped, food stolen, entertainers beaten)
danger_signals.meaning: Low park quality/unhappy visitors attract vandals and thugs; park safety compromised
Lack of staff/handymen indicates maintenance failure and declining park condition

danger_signals.visual_form: Missing staff members, operational gaps in park functions
danger_signals.meaning: Failed negotiations or wage issues create service breakdown
Poor park quality, ride failures, or inadequate facilities indicate world-state deterioration

safety_signals.visual_form: Rides in good cosmetic and mechanical condition with regular maintenance
safety_signals.meaning: Proper staff allocation and maintenance budget indicate safe, functional attractions

safety_signals.visual_form: Well-kept park grounds with trees, fountains, and tidy pathways
safety_signals.meaning: Adequate handymen and facilities staff indicate healthy park environment

safety_signals.visual_form: Guests remaining in park, visiting attractions, purchasing goods
safety_signals.meaning: Satisfied customers indicate good park design, pricing, and management

safety_signals.visual_form: Security guards visible patrolling park areas
safety_signals.meaning: Active security staff prevent vandalism and crime, maintain safety

safety_signals.visual_form: Shows and performers (clowns, medieval acts) operating throughout park
safety_signals.meaning: Active entertainment attracts visitors and improves park atmosphere/satisfaction

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Rival parks competing for visitors and market dominance
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player-designed park layout, custom ride placement, themed areas based on player choices

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Vandals and thugs (hostile visitors) who damage park when satisfaction low
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Crowd density, visitor distribution across attractions, purchasing behavior patterns

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Staff on strike (withheld labor) when negotiations fail
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Staff type visual differentiation (entertainers, security guards, mechanics, handymen) by role indicators

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Competing parks measured against player performance at year-end
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Annual performance rankings, trophy awards for achievements, market competition indicators

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visitors present in park, attending attractions, shopping, long queues at popular rides
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Guests remain in park longer, repeat visits increase, positive word-of-mouth

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Empty park areas, short queues, visitors leaving, closed/abandoned sections
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Visitors depart via bus, vandals/thugs appear to damage park, staff may strike

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Rides show visual wear, explode if neglected, messy footpaths, broken scenery
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ride failures cause guest injuries/deaths, maintenance staff become overwhelmed

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple shops and rides operational, new attractions under construction, park expansion
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased visitor arrivals via bus, research progresses faster, loans available for expansion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visual environmental change (weather effects, terrain type), lower land values visually represented
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Reduced visitor spending power, lower entry price tolerance, slower revenue growth

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Missing staff members at key stations, messy pathways, rides under-maintained, security gaps
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Park cleanliness declines, crime increases, ride breakdowns accelerate, guest satisfaction falls

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: New ride types and facilities become visible in available shop, research upgrades visible on rides
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Larger buses arrive with more capacity, rides become more durable, staff efficiency improves visually

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Balloons popping, food carts damaged, entertainers knocked down, graffiti/destruction visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Thugs visibly moving through park committing crimes, guest fear/flight response, property damage animation

environmental_storytelling.example: Different park settings (desert, glacier, UK terrain) with distinct visual characteristics communicate economic and climate challenges specific to world region
Visual rust, wear, and eventual explosion of unmaintained rides shows consequence timeline for neglect without explicit warning
Visitor distribution patterns and queue lengths at attractions visually communicate which rides/shops are successful and generating revenue
Visible janitors cleaning paths, security guards patrolling, entertainers performing show presence or absence tells story of park management quality
Trees, fountains, and decorative scenery placement visually demonstrate player investment in park appeal and guest experience quality
Damaged park assets (destroyed balloons, overturned food carts, hurt entertainers) remain visible, showing consequences of low satisfaction and inadequate security

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Arrive via bus, enter park, visit multiple attractions, purchase from shops/food stalls, remain extended time
Arrive via bus but quickly depart, avoid attractions, congregate at exits, board return bus prematurely
Appear in park, pop balloons, steal food, beat up entertainers, visibly damage property
Operate continuously, attract queues, generate revenue, show no visual degradation
Show visual wear progression, eventually explode (mechanical failure), become unusable
Perform assigned roles (cleaning, maintenance, entertainment, security), actively visible throughout park
Absent from posts, strike action prevents operations, services cease until negotiation resolves
Visible executing shows (clown acts, medieval performances), attract crowd gatherings, improve park atmosphere
Visibly beaten by thugs, become unavailable for performance, park entertainment value drops
Patrol park visibly, prevent/stop vandalism acts, maintain order
Unable to patrol all areas, vandalism occurs unchecked, crime escalates

notes: Theme Park communicates world-state primarily through consequence visualization rather than explicit UI warnings. The game uses a degradation timeline system: neglected systems (rides, paths, staff) show progressive visual decline before failure. Faction tension is implicit—rivals are shown at year-end judgment, and vandals/thugs represent guest dissatisfaction made manifest. Economic world-state (location prosperity/hardship) is conveyed through terrain visual variation and affects visitor purchasing power indirectly. The game teaches resource allocation through failure consequences: players learn danger signals by observing what happens when maintenance budgets are cut or staff inadequate.
